check: Every page has a unique, descriptive title tag under ~60 characters
description: Title tags are unique per page, describe the specific page content (not a generic site-wide template), and stay within the length search engines typically display in full
why_it_matters: Duplicate or missing titles across pages is a common, high-impact technical SEO issue that directly affects both ranking relevance and click-through rate from search results

check: Primary keyword/topic appears naturally near the front of the title
description: The page's core topic is reflected early in the title tag, phrased naturally rather than keyword-stuffed
why_it_matters: Search engines weight earlier terms in a title somewhat more heavily, and users scanning results also read left-to-right — this is a small, cheap optimization that compounds across every page

check: Every page has a unique, compelling meta description under ~155-160 characters
description: Meta descriptions are written per-page (not templated identically across many pages) and summarize the page's actual value in a way that encourages a click
why_it_matters: Meta descriptions aren't a direct ranking factor but materially affect click-through rate from the results page they appear on — an uncompelling or duplicate description leaves ranking potential on the table

check: Single H1 per page, matching the page's actual topic
description: Each page has exactly one H1 that accurately describes the page's primary topic — this is `QA033`'s heading-hierarchy requirement with its SEO framing added
why_it_matters: Multiple or missing H1s create ambiguity about a page's primary topic for both users and search engines, and often correlate with deeper heading-hierarchy problems

check: Heading hierarchy reflects genuine content structure, not visual styling choices
description: H2s, H3s, etc. are used to reflect actual content organization (sections, subsections), not selected because of how a heading level happens to look visually
why_it_matters: Using heading levels purely for visual size (styling an H4 to look bigger because a design needs that size, rather than because it's semantically a subsection) undermines both accessibility (`QA033`) and the structural signal search engines use to understand content

check: URLs are short, readable, and reflect content hierarchy
description: URLs use readable words (not IDs/parameters alone) and reflect a logical hierarchy matching the site structure, e.g. /blog/topic-name rather than /p?id=48291
why_it_matters: Readable URLs are easier for users to evaluate before clicking, easier to remember/share, and give search engines an additional (weak but real) relevance signal

check: URLs use hyphens to separate words, not underscores or spaces
description: Word separation in URLs consistently uses hyphens (-), which search engines parse as word boundaries, unlike underscores which are sometimes treated as a single token
why_it_matters: This is a small, easy-to-get-wrong-once-and-propagate-everywhere convention — worth codifying in a URL-generation utility rather than leaving to per-page manual entry

check: Descriptive, keyword-relevant file names for images
description: Image file names describe the image content (e.g. red-running-shoes.jpg rather than IMG_4821.jpg) before upload
why_it_matters: File names are a minor but real image-search relevance signal, and this is essentially free to get right if established as a pre-upload convention

check: Alt text is descriptive and SEO-relevant, not keyword-stuffed
description: Alt text (required for accessibility per `QA030`) also reasonably describes the image in a way that supports topical relevance, without unnaturally cramming keywords
why_it_matters: Alt text serves both accessibility and SEO simultaneously when written well — keyword-stuffed alt text fails the accessibility purpose and reads as manipulative to search engines besides

check: Descriptive anchor text used for internal links, not generic 'click here'
description: Internal links use anchor text that describes the destination page's topic, giving both users and crawlers context about what the linked page is about
why_it_matters: Generic anchor text ('click here', 'read more') wastes a genuinely useful relevance signal that descriptive anchor text provides essentially for free

check: Important pages receive proportionally more internal links than minor pages
description: The site's internal linking pattern reflects actual page importance — cornerstone/pillar content receives more internal links than a minor supporting page, not a flat uniform linking pattern
why_it_matters: Internal links are one of the strongest signals a site controls directly for communicating relative page importance to search engines — an undifferentiated linking pattern wastes this lever entirely

check: New content is linked from relevant existing pages upon publish
description: When new content is published, at least a few contextually relevant existing pages are updated to link to it, rather than relying solely on the new page appearing in a blog index/sitemap
why_it_matters: Waiting for organic internal links to accumulate naturally leaves new content under-linked and slower to be crawled/understood for weeks or months longer than necessary

check: Content answers the query's actual search intent (informational/transactional/navigational/commercial)
description: Page content and format match what a searcher for the target query actually wants — a transactional query gets a page that supports purchasing, an informational query gets genuinely informative content, not a mismatch between intent and page type
why_it_matters: This is search-intent alignment, one of the strongest determinants of ranking success independent of technical optimization — a technically perfect page for the wrong intent still won't rank well

check: Content is structured with scannable formatting (short paragraphs, lists, subheadings)
description: Long-form content uses subheadings, bullet/numbered lists, and reasonably short paragraphs rather than dense unbroken text blocks
why_it_matters: Scannable formatting improves both user engagement (reducing bounce) and passage-level extractability for AI Overviews/featured snippets, which favor clearly delineated, directly-answering passages

check: The primary question/topic is answered directly and early in the content
description: For content targeting a specific question or informational query, a direct, complete answer appears within the first few sentences/paragraph, with elaboration following
why_it_matters: This directly supports featured-snippet and AI-Overview extraction — these surfaces tend to pull from content that answers clearly and early rather than building up to an answer through narrative structure

check: Content covers the topic with genuine depth, not a thin restatement of the query
description: Page content demonstrates real depth/completeness on its topic rather than being a shallow, keyword-targeted page with minimal actual information
why_it_matters: Thin content is both a poor user experience and a specific, named quality signal search engines evaluate — this remains one of the most consistent drivers of ranking failure for otherwise technically sound pages

check: No two pages targeting the same primary query without a clear differentiation
description: The site doesn't have multiple pages competing for the same primary search intent without a deliberate reason (e.g. old content not consolidated with new)
why_it_matters: Cannibalization splits ranking signal across competing pages instead of concentrating it on one strong page, often resulting in neither page ranking as well as a single consolidated page would

check: Overlapping content is consolidated or clearly differentiated by intent
description: When multiple pages do legitimately cover related territory, each targets a genuinely distinct sub-intent, or the weaker/older page is consolidated (301'd) into the stronger one
why_it_matters: Periodic content audits that identify and resolve cannibalization typically recover ranking performance that was being split across competing pages

check: Page CTAs match the content's actual search intent stage
description: A page targeting an early-stage informational query doesn't push an aggressive 'buy now' CTA mismatched to where that searcher actually is in their journey
why_it_matters: A CTA/intent mismatch hurts conversion rate even on pages that are otherwise ranking and getting traffic successfully — the SEO win is wasted if the page doesn't convert the intent it earned

check: Tables, lists, and comparison content structured for extraction by featured snippets/AI answers
description: Comparison, list, and definition-style content uses actual table/list HTML markup (not styled divs) so it's structurally extractable
why_it_matters: Search engines and AI answer engines specifically favor genuinely structured markup (real <table>, <ol>/<ul>) over visually-similar but semantically generic markup when extracting content for snippets/summaries

check: Content is original, not substantially duplicated from another source (including AI-generated boilerplate)
description: Published content offers genuine original value/perspective rather than being a lightly-reworded aggregation of other sources or generic, unedited AI-generated text
why_it_matters: Both duplicate content and detectably generic/unedited AI content correlate with weaker ranking performance — the fix isn't avoiding AI assistance entirely, it's ensuring genuine editorial value and originality in what ships

check: Paginated series use consistent, crawlable navigation, not infinite scroll alone
description: Multi-page content series (blog archives, product listings) provide crawlable pagination links, even if infinite scroll is also offered for user experience
why_it_matters: Infinite-scroll-only pagination can prevent crawlers from discovering content beyond the first page, since crawlers don't reliably trigger scroll-based loading the way a human user does

check: Meaningful content visible above the fold, not just navigation/hero imagery
description: The initial visible viewport includes some genuine content relevant to the page's topic, not solely a full-viewport hero image/navigation with no text
why_it_matters: Google's page-layout algorithm has historically penalized pages with too little above-the-fold content relative to ads/navigation — meaningful content near the top also improves initial user engagement signals
